violations.text: At the time of inspection, several foods in the Sandwich Prep Unit were observed at 43 to 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Food items were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025

At the time of inspection, the Sandwich Prep Unit was observed in need of repair/adjustment... - The unit was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 38 to 48 degrees Fahrenheit with food temperatures at 43 to 44 degrees Fahrenheit. (Unit was adjusted to colder holding temperature at the time of inspection.) - The gaskets to the doors of the sandwich prep unit were observed to be torn Equipment is to maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the deli slicer were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025
The tiles in the kitchen area is in poor repair (cracked/chipped). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2026

The basement storage shelving as well as the side office and utility areas were observed to have an accumulation of old debris and droppings behind equipment. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025

Several food items in the Sandwich Prep Unit were observed held at 44-45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per the PIC, the hoods to the prep unit were open for a lunch rush. Food items were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Working containers used for storing POISONOUS OR TOXIC MATERIALS such as cleaners and SANITIZERS taken from bulk supplies shall be clearly and individually identified with the common name of the material. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024
The condensate from the sandwich prep refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2024

The basement storage area as well as the side office and utility areas were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024
The tiles in the kitchen area is in poor repair (cracked/chipped). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by accumulation of droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
Dead rodents were observed in the basement. Dead rodents/pests must be removed from the premises. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by new droppings observed. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

Multiple dead rodents were observed in various locations of the establishment (On glue pad in office area, on glue pad in corner of basement by water heater, on the floor underneath shelving in basement, on glue pad next to old chest freezer.) The establishment shall remove dead rodents from the facility. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

Unnecessary items (Boxes of old receipts/paperwork) were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Owner stated that they would dispose of any unnecessary items/organize the basement area. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by a large accumulation of droppings throughout the entire facility. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2023
Multiple dead rodents were observed in the basement/office area of the establishment. The establishment shall remove dead rodents from the facility. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2023

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2023
